name: Sammy Cahn
abstract: Samuel Cohen (June 18, 1913 – January 15, 1993), known professionally as Sammy Cahn, was an American lyricist, songwriter, and musician. He is best known for his romantic lyrics to films and Broadway songs, as well as stand-alone songs premiered by recording companies in the Greater Los Angeles area.

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I was accompanying Sammy Cahn who was a songwriter of some repute... So one day Sam called and he’d say, “oh we’ve got this gig in Chicago.”... So we had some arrangements... we went over it... we played all this stuff for Sam.
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Sammy Cahn wrote the lyrics to that song... Sammy Cahn has, inadvertently, of course, written, or used the same title that he had used for another song, with which, you know, that he had collaborated with Nicholas Bronsky on.

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: And we got the original Nelson Riddle charts and Count Basie charts and Sammy Cahn? No. Another one of his bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got a whole thing over here, there’s Johnny Mercer, Sammy Cahn, Jerome Kern.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]
